DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT ALPHA MEDIA GROUP, LLC, VDM GROUP, INC., VLAD MYASNYANKIN, GK GROUP, INC., YEVGENIY KHIZVER, TRACE CAPITAL HOLDINGS, INC., TIMOTHY EUN, AND GARY KAHN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, the demurrer is sustained in part with leave to amend the third cause of action. Defendant portrays plaintiff's entire first amended complaint as a sham pleading. Plaintiff has failed to adequately plead the third cause of action for rescission based on economic duress. As defendant correctly points out, plaintiff has raised an entirely new set of facts for pleading economic duress and has failed to provide a sufficient explanation for how or why the new facts relate to the facts originally pled. Thus, the court will disregard the inconsistent allegations and read into the amended complaint the allegations of the original complaint. See Owens v. Kings Supermarket (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 379, 384. The third cause of action for economic duress, as stated in the original complaint, does not adequately plead facts establishing that Plaintiff had no reasonable alternative but to agree to an unfavorable contract. See CrossTalk Productions, Inc. v. Jacobson (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 631, 644. The demurrer is sustained as to the third cause of action with ten days leave to amend. Contrary to defendant's position, plaintiff has adequately pled the second cause of action for rescission based on fraudulent inducement. The plaintiff has provided adequate explanations for the differences between the facts alleged in the original complaint and the facts pled in the first amendment complaint. Because Polaris has stated a cause of action for rescission based on fraudulent inducement, the release in the MIPA is arguably invalid, and therefore, Polaris can maintain its breach of contract claims, fraud claim, breach of fiduciary duty claim, and accounting claim. Furthermore, the plaintiff has pled a derivative suit on behalf of the Alpha Media Group shareholders in compliance with Cal. Corp. Code 800. Additionally, the court will grant plaintiff with ten days leave to amend the complaint to add defendants Gene Khizver, GK Group, Inc., Garry Kann, and Trace Capital Holding, Inc. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Defendants' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Control Date)
Off Calendar Jun-12-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Samuel G. Lockhart, of Lockhart Law Firm, APC., Tel. 951.461.8878, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. DEFENDANTS ALPHA MEDIA GROUP, LLC, VDM GROUP, INC., VLAD MYASNYANKIN, GK GROUP, INC., YEVGENIY KHIZVER, TRACE CAPITAL HOLDINGS, INC., TIMOTHY EUN, AND GARY KAHN'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted as to all causes of action other than the eighth cause of action with twenty days leave to amend and as to the eighth cause of action for civil conspiracy without leave to amend. Civil conspiracy is not a cause of action. (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 510-11.) However, Plaintiff may amend the complaint to add civil conspiracy allegations. The complaint does not adequately plead facts establishing fraud or economic duress, which Plaintiff argues in opposition are the grounds for the rescission cause of action. Because the rescission cause of action has not been adequately pled, the breach of contract, intentional misrepresentation, negligent misrepresentation, breach of fiduciary duty, breach of oral contract, and oral book account causes of action are barred by the release in the Membership Interest Purchase Agreement ("MIPA"). (Complaint, Ex. B, MIPA, par. 5.) The second cause of action for economic duress does not adequately plead facts establishing that Plaintiff had no reasonable alternative but to agree to an unfavorable contract. (CrossTalk Productions, Inc. v. Jacobson (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 631, 644.) The sixth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty must be brought as a derivative action, as the complaint alleges harm to all shareholders of Alpha Media Group. (Jones v. H.F. Ahmanson & Co. (1969) 1 Cal.3d 93, 107.) The fifth cause of action for accounting does not adequately plead that an uncertain amount is owed to Plaintiff. To the extent that Plaintiff is asserting that it needs an accounting because of a breach of fiduciary duty, the cause of action must be brought derivatively. Order signed in open court. Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/HK)
